spec.goal: Define the normative post-In-Review lifecycle for Maestro-owned lanes using openai/symphony as the only external design standard and the local owned-lane policy plus workflow adapters as execution constraints.
success_criteria: A new spec document defines the authoritative post-In-Review states, decisions, transitions, and ownership boundaries for Maestro-owned lanes.
The spec aligns with the current runtime handoff boundary and the owned-lane policy without turning current skill names into the core domain model.
The spec maps the accepted lifecycle to the existing follow-on implementation issues XY-173, XY-174, XY-175, and XY-177.
constraints: Treat openai/symphony README.md and SPEC.md as the only external design standard.
Use AGENTS.md and checked-in skills only as local execution-context inputs and operating constraints.
Stay in spec scope; do not implement the runtime in this issue.

tasks.title: Synthesize governing inputs
tasks.objective: Capture the current Maestro runtime boundary, owned-lane policy, local workflow adapter constraints, and latest Symphony service principles that should shape the post-In-Review lifecycle.
outputs: A grounded design frame for the new lifecycle spec
verification: Collected sources are reflected in the written spec without placeholder claims

tasks.title: Write the post-In-Review lifecycle spec
tasks.objective: Author the normative spec covering wait, repair, land, closeout, cleanup, manual intervention, and automatic recovery after PR-backed In Review handoff.
verification: State transitions and ownership boundaries are explicit
Failure and retry handling after In Review are defined

tasks.title: Integrate and verify the new spec
tasks.objective: Link the new spec from the spec index, reference the existing follow-on issues, and validate the plan/spec artifacts.
inputs: existing follow-on issues XY-173, XY-174, XY-175, XY-177

triggers: Symphony evidence materially changes the intended lifecycle boundary
The accepted lifecycle requires new follow-on issue splits beyond XY-173/174/175/177
Local runtime or owned-lane policy evidence contradicts the drafted state machine

evidence: 2026-03-19: Reviewed the current Maestro handoff boundary in system_maestro_runtime, the owned-lane decision classes in system_maestro_owned_lane_policy, the local workflow adapter contracts in AGENTS.md and checked-in skills, and the latest openai/symphony README.md plus SPEC.md as the only external design standard.

2026-03-19: Added docs/spec/system_maestro_post_review_lifecycle.md to define the normative lane phases after PR-backed In Review handoff: review_wait, review_repair, ready_to_land, landing, delivery_closeout, and cleanup, along with retry, cancellation, and manual-intervention boundaries.
2026-03-19: Updated docs/spec/index.md to route readers to the new post-In-Review lifecycle spec and mapped the accepted implementation split onto XY-173, XY-174, XY-175, and XY-177.

2026-03-19: Fresh verification passed: cargo make fmt-check, python3 /Users/xavier/.codex/skills/plan-writing/scripts/validate_plan_contract.py --path docs/plans/2026-03-20_maestro-xy-160-post-in-review-lifecycle.json, python3 /Users/xavier/.codex/skills/plan-execution/scripts/read_plan_contract.py --path docs/plans/2026-03-20_maestro-xy-160-post-in-review-lifecycle.json, git diff --check.

2026-03-20: Review-repair updated the lifecycle spec to add an explicit pre-merge cancellation path into cleanup, define deterministic successful completed-state resolution for delivery_closeout, and add the missing ready_to_land downgrade transitions back to review_wait or review_repair.

2026-03-20: Follow-up review-repair promoted the post-merge success target into the workflow contract as tracker.completed_state, restricted direct cleanup to terminal pre-merge cancellation, and added PR open-versus-closed state to the authoritative signals shared by the owned-lane and post-review lifecycle specs.
